Unlike static word embeddings provided by `NLEmbedding`, contextual embeddings dynamically adjust based on surrounding words, enabling deeper language comprehension.

Some models support multiple languages; for example, you can use the same object to compute embeddings for both English and Spanish. # NLContextualEmbedding

**Class**

A model that computes sequences of embedding vectors for natural language utterances.

## Declaration

```swift
class NLContextualEmbedding
```

## Overview

`NLContextualEmbedding` provides a dense vector representation of natural language utterances using models trained with contextualized language understanding. Unlike static word embeddings provided by `NLEmbedding`, contextual embeddings dynamically adjust based on surrounding words, enabling deeper language comprehension.

If you are building text classification or word tagging models, you can use `NLContextualEmbedding` with [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CreateML] by selecting [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CreateML/MLTextClassifier/FeatureExtractorType/bertEmbedding] as the feature extraction component in your model. For more information on how to create your own text classification model refer to [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CreateML/creating-a-text-classifier-model].



Before configuring your contextual embedding:

- Select a model that’s appropriate for your target language when initializing the embedding.
- Call [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLContextualEmbedding/requestAssets(completionHandler:)] before computing the embedding to confirm assets are available.

Some models support multiple languages; for example, you can use the same object to compute embeddings for both English and Spanish. However, this isn’t always the case — languages like English and Chinese may require separate models. For more information about supported languages, refer to [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLContextualEmbedding/languages].

## Creating a contextual embedding

- **init(modelIdentifier:)**: Creates a contextual embedding from a model identifier.
- **init(language:)**: Creates a contextual embedding from a language.
- **init(script:)**: Creates a contextual embedding from a script.

## Discovering available contextual embeddings

- **contextualEmbeddings(forValues:)**: Returns contextual embedding models that match the specified language, script, or revision criteria.

## Inspecting the contextual embedding

- **dimension**: The dimensionality of the embedding vectors generated by the model.
- **hasAvailableAssets**: A Boolean value that indicates whether assets are available on-device.
- **languages**: The languages that the contextual embedding supports.
- **maximumSequenceLength**: The maximum number of tokens in sequence for which the embedding generates vectors.
- **modelIdentifier**: A string that uniquely identifies the embedding model.
- **revision**: The version number the contextual embedding uses.
- **scripts**: The writing systems that the language uses.

## Requesting assets

- **requestAssets(completionHandler:)**: Requests embedding model assets and downloads them if available.
- **NLContextualEmbedding.AssetsResult**: The availability of the contextual embedding model assets.

## Loading and unloading assets

- **load()**: The instance method that loads the embedding model.
- **unload()**: The instance method that unloads the embedding model.

## Applying an embedding

- **embeddingResult(for:language:)**: Applies an embedding to a string and obtains the resulting embedding vectors.
- **NLContextualEmbeddingResult**: An object that represents the embedding vector result from applying a contextual embedding to a string.

## Contextual embedding

- **NLContextualEmbeddingKey**: This class defines properties that you can filter or search for contextual embeddings.
- **NLScript**: The writing scripts that the Natural Language framework supports.

## Inherits From

- NSObject

## Conforms To

- CVarArg
- CustomDebugStringConvertible
- CustomStringConvertible
- Equatable
- Hashable
- NSObjectProtocol
